**Headline state (2026-05-19 morning).** Both arcs in deep-refactor mode per Daniel 07:13Z. OneThird: paper-axiom-close requires FULL REFACTOR of Lean to proof-by-contradiction matching paper architecture (mg-d8c7 SCOPING filed). **Daniel directive 2026-05-19T07:13Z (verbatim)**: "you should be mailing me for input on your projects, not mayor. Frankl I still want more forking. Sounds like 1/3-2/3 the lean isn't set up right at all for our proof yet so we need to refactor until it is. I am still looking for ways to make things more manageable, since polecats etc seem to have trouble wrangling and reasoning about these proofs."

Both refactor directives translated to scoping tickets (mg-ea01 Frankl cascade-fork; mg-d8c7 OneThird FULL REFACTOR). Channel correction noted: PM mails Daniel for project input, not mayor (feedback_pm_is_primary_channel_to_daniel saved).

## Trajectory (vs Daniel 07:13Z bar)

**Strategic state.** Both arcs are publication-defensible TODAY via interim disclosure-pivot (mg-1b2b GREEN + mg-234e GREEN + mg-be48 GREEN-Tier-A). Daniel could greenlight Zulip post-interim per current state. But Daniel 18:05Z + 07:13Z raises bar: paper axioms must close. Two parallel multi-session refactor arcs now scoping (mg-ea01 + mg-d8c7).

**Vacuity cascade**: 7 discoveries in 60h, bottomed out at substantive content. Audit-before-build at Phase D Checkpoints prevented multi-M-token sinks at mg-5fbd S7-F (saved ~2M tokens). Pattern: each audit sharpened the architectural model. Daniel's persistent skepticism was right.

**Polecat-manageability concern** (Daniel 07:13Z). Recurring patterns:
- Polecat human-dev biases (mg-8dce extrapolated "multi-month" from local failures; AI-native cascade is days-to-weeks not months) — addressed via AI-native-dev framing + feedback_polecat_human_dev_bias memory.
- Polecat scoping-then-execute pattern prevents framing-vacuity (mg-5fbd Phase D Checkpoint 3 caught 7th vacuity; mg-7e53 RED Phase B caught structural-impossibility before cascade-fork attempt).
- PROOF-STRUCTURE-ONBOARDING.md as READ-FIRST canonical reduces re-derivation.

**Cumulative spend**: ~25M tokens across both arcs in last 48h. Most expensive: 10 Z2 sub-splits + 7 audit cycles. Worth the architectural clarity (vacuity-cascade discovery prevented sinking 10-20M tokens into vacuous paths).

**Risk watchlist**:
- mg-ea01 sub-ticket sub-split risk: Z2 went 10 sub-splits + AMBER-final; cascade-fork sub-tickets may exhibit same; pre-authorised contingency.
- mg-d8c7 budget projection: 7-12M / 5-7 months is HIGH risk on Daniel's "make things more manageable" directive — scoping output may surface decomposition that improves manageability.
- TC-diamond bicomplex bypass at mg-ea01 Sub-ticket 2: if namespace-rename doesn't bypass diamond, escalate immediately (don't sink Sub-tickets 3-6 budget).
- Polecat-manageability fundamental: even with all process improvements, multi-month proofs may exceed AI-current-state capability — Daniel may need to decide between "ship interim disclosure-pivot now" and "wait 5-7 months for axiom-close".
